name: Castillo de Turón – running circular desde Ardales (PR-A 91)
region_intro: Jogging routes around Ardales traverse a landscape defined by dramatic natural features in Málaga, Spain. The region features the deep Gaitanes Gorge carved by the Guadalhorce River, alongside a beautiful lake district formed by three interconnected reservoirs. Joggers can explore varied terrain, from the rugged limestone formations of the Sierra de la Huma to the more gentle paths along the Guadalhorce and Turón rivers. This diverse environment offers a range of running experiences across its hills and valleys. Best jogging routes around Ardales The most popular jogging route is Castillo de Turón – running circular desde Ardales (PR-A 91), an 8.3 miles (13.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 44 minutes to complete. This route leads to the historic ruins of Castillo de Turón, offering panoramic views of the surrounding area. Another top favourite among local runners is Caminito del Rey loop from Pantano del Chorro, a difficult 3.8 miles (6.1 km) path. This route provides views of the dramatic Gaitanes Gorge and the surrounding mountain environment. Local runners also love the Mirador del Embalses loop from Peñarrubia, a 4.6 miles (7.3 km) trail leading through the scenic lake district, often completed in about 53 minutes. Jogging around Ardales is defined by dramatic gorges, serene lake districts, and varied mountainous terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from challenging climbs to more relaxed riverside paths. The routes in Ardales are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 500 runners have used komoot to explore Ardales's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Ardales?
A: Ardales offers a diverse selection of over 10 running routes, catering to various fitness levels. While many are challenging, there are also moderate options for those seeking a less strenuous experience.

Q: Are there any easy jogging routes suitable for beginners in Ardales?
A: While most routes in Ardales are rated as difficult, there are a few moderate options that beginners might find more manageable. These trails often feature less elevation gain and provide a good introduction to the region's natural beauty. For a less strenuous option, consider trails leading to the historic Cueva de Ardales, which offer historical context and views.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails around Ardales?
A: The jogging trails in Ardales offer a varied terrain. You'll find dramatic mountain gorges, serene paths along three interconnected reservoirs, and rugged limestone formations in areas like the Sierra de la Huma. Expect a mix of challenging climbs and more relaxed riverside paths, providing a dynamic running experience.

Q: Are there any circular running trails in Ardales?
A: Yes, Ardales features several excellent circular running trails. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996262953/castillo-de-turon-running-circular-desde-ardales-pr-a-91" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Turón – running circular desde Ardales (PR-A 91)&lt;/a&gt;, which is 8.3 miles long and leads to historic ruins with panoramic views. Another great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19322472" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Embalses loop from Peñarrubia&lt;/a&gt;, a 4.6-mile trail through the scenic lake district.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see while running in Ardales?
A: Many running routes in Ardales offer stunning views of local landmarks. You can run past the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4437799" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gaitanejo Reservoir Dam&lt;/a&gt;, or catch glimpses of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3782093" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Conde de Guadalhorce Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. Trails also lead to historic sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1121683" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de la Peña de Ardales&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1121681" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turón Castle&lt;/a&gt;, offering a blend of nature and history.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about the trails in Ardales?
A: The running trails in Ardales are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars. Runners frequently praise the dramatic gorges, serene lake districts, and varied mountainous terrain. Many appreciate the diverse options available, from challenging climbs to more relaxed riverside paths, and the breathtaking views of the natural landscape.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer views of the famous Caminito del Rey?
A: Yes, several running routes provide spectacular views of the Caminito del Rey and the Gaitanes Gorge. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21828197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caminito del Rey loop from Pantano del Chorro&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 3.8-mile path that offers direct views of this dramatic area. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10298328" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lookout Caminito del Rey – View of Caminito del Rey loop from Bobastro&lt;/a&gt;, which provides panoramic vistas.

Q: Can I find long-distance running trails in Ardales?
A: Ardales is home to several long-distance running trails for those seeking a greater challenge. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40013421" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse Tajo de la Encantada – Balcon de cristal loop from Carratraca&lt;/a&gt; spans over 8.8 miles (14.3 km) with significant elevation, offering a demanding and rewarding experience through the region's stunning landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Ardales?
A: Ardales offers good jogging conditions for much of the year due to its Mediterranean climate. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures for running. Summers can be hot, making early morning or late evening runs preferable, especially on shaded trails or near the lakes. Winters are mild, allowing for comfortable running, though some higher elevation trails might be cooler.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by the Ardales Lakes?
A: Absolutely! The area around Ardales is famous for its three interconnected reservoirs: the Conde de Guadalhorce, Guadalhorce, and Guadalteba. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19322472" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Embalses loop from Peñarrubia&lt;/a&gt; lead directly through this scenic lake district, offering peaceful jogs with beautiful contrasts of water, green trees, and blue skies. You can also find views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5764877" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Three Reservoirs Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any specific trails that offer panoramic viewpoints?
A: Yes, Ardales is rich in panoramic viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996262953/castillo-de-turon-running-circular-desde-ardales-pr-a-91" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Turón – running circular desde Ardales (PR-A 91)&lt;/a&gt; trail leads to the ruins of Castillo de Turón, providing expansive views of Ardales and its surroundings. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5764877" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Three Reservoirs Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; offers stunning vistas of the lake district, perfect for a scenic run.

Q: What are the general difficulty levels of running routes in Ardales?
A: The running routes in Ardales primarily consist of difficult trails, making them ideal for experienced runners seeking a challenge. There are also a few moderate routes available, which provide a good balance for those looking for a less intense but still engaging run through the region's beautiful landscapes.
tips: Here is a bridge over the Turón River dating from the 1st century BC. C. Although it only conserves three of its five original arches, it is one of the few Roman bridges that still allows the passage of vehicles and pedestrians today.

The castle originally had more than ten watchtowers that served as support for the castle of Ardales during the Nasrid period. What remains of the wall was built on the limestone cliff, which made the castle a strategic and impregnable point.
